cards.text: Dead-eye staring contest. First to blink, laugh, or beg is the coward this room always suspected.
Thumb war to the death. Loser stays silent the entire next round. Winner gets to gloat about it until sunrise.
Hype-man showdown: ten seconds each introducing your opponent like a walkout. Loudest, most unhinged intro wins.
Slow-motion duel: act out your most embarrassing fall of the year, frame by frame. The room judges commitment.
Fake-cry-off. Fifteen seconds to produce real grief. Dry at the bell loses; both dry, the room picks the sadder fraud.
Villain monologue: explain out loud why this friend group had it coming. Best menace wins; first giggle loses.
Air-instrument solo. Pick your weapon, commit like your soul is the ante. The least committed loses it.
Ten seconds of trash talk about your opponent's taste in music. All bark, zero facts. Better mouth takes the belt.
Seduce this table. No music, no touching, no mercy. Whoever makes the room more uncomfortable takes the belt.
Worst-impression duel: your meanest impression of each other, to each other's face. The one that still lands wins.
Wall-sit-off. Backs to the wall, thighs level, hands off. First one to slide, stand, or whimper loses.
Plank on the floor while the room reads your worst decisions back to you. First knee down loses.
Stand on one foot, eyes shut, while the room insults your judgment. First foot to touch down loses.
Straight-face duel. The room may do anything but touch you. First to smile loses; two stone faces split the belt.
Arms straight out to the sides, palms up, no bending. The room narrates your failures. First elbow to drop loses.
Total silence while the room interrogates you both. First sound out of either of you loses the belt.
The room says nice things about you and means them. First to smile, deflect, or say thanks loses.
Pull the worst face you own and hold it. No adjusting, no laughing. First face to relax loses.
Hold one note. Same pitch, no cheating a breath. Last voice still going takes the belt.
Full belly laugh at nothing, nonstop, straight at each other. First to go quiet or turn real loses.
Fifteen seconds each narrating the other like wildlife on a nature program. Break them first and it is yours.
Argue over which of you this group would replace first. Neither of you may say the word 'I'. First slip loses.
Say each other's full name back and forth, more disappointed every round. First one to laugh loses.
Both talk at once, both defending your own honor, neither allowed to stop. First to go quiet or crack loses.
Apologize to this room nonstop, no repeats, no fiction. First one to run out of sins loses.
Freeze exactly as you are. The room may say anything, touch nothing. First twitch, scratch, or shift of weight loses.
One leads, one mirrors every move. Fifteen seconds, then swap. First to fall behind loses the belt.
Threaten each other. No volume, no profanity, no touching. The room crowns whichever calm was scarier.
Flinch duel. Fake swings only, hands stay a foot apart, contact forfeits. First to flinch loses.
Deliver your opponent's eulogy while they sit there breathing. The room crowns whichever one hurt worse.
Sell your opponent to this room as a used item. You must disclose every known defect. Best pitch wins.
Recruit this room into your cult, fifteen seconds each. Whoever gets more hands in the air takes the belt.
